content: We can frame Self-Refine as the incremental "tactical" refinement step and Reflexion as the strategic "memory-grounded" reflection step, creating a two-layer autonomous improvement framework that retains Self-Refine’s no-training advantage while fixing its limitation of lacking long-term learning across tasks. This hybrid framework would let an LLM accumulate generalisable improvement patterns from past self-refinement cycles instead of re-starting from scratch for every new generation task.
